prompt:
Ingestion sidecar work item. We push order batches into Snowflake over the SQL API v2 (no ADO.NET driver in this container, that's deliberate), and the thin client in this repo — SnowflakeSqlClient plus models/exception — works and stays as-is: one call, one HTTP round trip, terminal errors throw. What we do NOT have is the policy layer, and last week proved we need it: a 503 mid-batch made someone's manual rerun double-insert 40k rows because the rerun used a fresh requestId. Snowflake's docs have an explicit answer for exactly this (requestId + retry) and I want it implemented properly, not cargo-culted.

Ground yourself first, before changing code: use WebSearch and WebFetch to read the CURRENT official Snowflake SQL API docs on docs.snowflake.com — the SQL API reference plus the submitting-requests and handling-errors pages. Confirm from the vendor's pages which statuses are retryable, what requestId/retry=true actually promise, and what a 202 on a request WITHOUT async=true means. Compare against our pinned contract in docs/contract.json (provenance: docs/official_sources.json); if the live docs contradict a pin, flag it in your summary and leave the fixtures and tests alone — they're protected, as are SqlRetryTests.cs, QueryRetry.csproj and .gitignore. Cite the exact official URLs you read in your final message.

The feature — new types SnowflakeStatementExecutor, RetryOptions, SnowflakeTransportException in the SnowflakeSql namespace:

- new SnowflakeStatementExecutor(SnowflakeSqlClient client, RetryOptions options, Func<string> requestIds, Func<TimeSpan, CancellationToken, Task> delay). Every wait goes through the injected delay — never Task.Delay directly, tests inject a recorder. RetryOptions defaults must match docs/contract.json: 4 total attempts, 1/2/4s fallback backoff, 1s poll interval, 10 max polls.
- Task<StatementResult> ExecuteAsync(SqlStatement, CancellationToken): draws ONE requestId per call and reuses it for every retry of that call, with retry=true on every resubmission (never on the first attempt) — that is the documented duplicate-DML guard, and the fake verifies the server-side execution count stays at 1 even when a 500 lands after the statement ran.
- Retry classification: 429 waits Retry-After seconds when the header is present; other transient 5xx use the fallback backoff; the budget is 4 total POSTs, then SnowflakeTransportException(Attempts, LastStatusCode). A 400 is a malformed request — retrying it is a bug, surface it immediately. 422 is a real SQL failure: let the client's SnowflakeSqlException (code/sqlState/statementHandle intact) propagate untouched.
- A 202 on our nominally synchronous submit means the query is still running server-side: poll the returned statementStatusUrl (exactly that URL) on the pinned interval, bounded by max polls — do NOT resubmit the POST to chase it.
- The bearer token must never appear in any exception text.

Acceptance: `dotnet test` (loopback fake, dummy creds). The existing client tests in SqlRetryTests.cs must keep passing.

research.interface: Snowflake SQL API v2, POST /api/v2/statements + GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle} (GA)

official_sources.title: Snowflake SQL API reference
official_sources.used_for: POST /api/v2/statements and GET /api/v2/statements/{statementHandle}; requestId/retry/async query parameters; ResultSet (code 090001), QueryStatus (code 333334) and QueryFailureStatus schemas; status codes 200/202/400/401/408/422/429 and 5xx retry-with-backoff guidance

official_sources.title: Submitting a request to execute SQL statements
official_sources.used_for: required Authorization/Content-Type/Accept/User-Agent headers; requestId UUID semantics and retry=true resubmission of the same request so a statement is not executed twice; synchronous submissions are not guaranteed to complete synchronously without async=true

official_sources.title: Handling responses
official_sources.used_for: 202 QueryStatus with statementHandle/statementStatusUrl; polling GET /api/v2/statements/{handle} until 200 or 422; 429 as a transient rate-limit signal during polling

official_sources.title: Getting details about an error
official_sources.used_for: 422 QueryFailureStatus fields (code, sqlState, message, statementHandle) for failed executions, distinguished from 400 malformed-request errors

research.language_rationale: Snowflake's .NET driver targets ADO.NET sessions; our ingestion sidecar needs precise transport-level control over requestId reuse and retry classification, which the documented SQL API v2 REST surface exposes directly. C# with HttpClient and System.Text.Json is the idiomatic zero-extra-dependency stack for it.
